metadata.name: ww-input-qr-code
metadata.description: A camera-based QR code scanner input component that reads QR codes in real-time using the device camera. Integrates with the WeWeb form system so scanned values can be submitted and validated like any other form field.
features: Real-time QR code detection via device camera using html5-qrcode
Camera selection via facingMode ('environment', 'user') or a specific device by label/id ('custom')
Form integration with fieldName, required and custom formula validation
Debounced scanning: the same code repeated within 1 second does not re-fire the event
Responsive camera viewport that refreshes on element resize
Exposes scanning status, last scanned value, available cameras and camera availability as variables
No auto-reset: status stays 'success' after a successful scan until the next scan event

properties.name: cameraSelection
properties.description: How the scanner picks which camera to use. 'environment' prefers the back camera, 'user' prefers the front camera, 'custom' uses the device matched by cameraName.

properties.name: cameraName
properties.description: The label or device ID of a specific camera to use. Only considered when cameraSelection is 'custom'. Inactive when cameraSelection is not 'custom'.

properties.name: fieldName
properties.description: The field name used to identify this scanner's value in form submissions. Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container (sidePanelContent.form?.uid is falsy).

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the scanner must have produced a scanned value before the enclosing form can be submitted. Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container (sidePanelContent.form?.uid is falsy).

properties.name: customValidation
properties.description: Enable custom validation formula for this scanner's value. Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container (sidePanelContent.form?.uid is falsy).

properties.name: validation
properties.description: Formula expression used for custom validation. Only used when customValidation is enabled. Must evaluate to a boolean. Inactive when the element is not in a form container or when customValidation is false.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container (sidePanelContent.form?.uid is falsy) or when customValidation is false.

events.name: scan
events.description: Triggered when a QR code is successfully scanned. Debounced: the same code repeated within 1 second does not re-fire.

localContext.description: Scanner state available to this element and any children placed inside it.

variables.name: code
variables.description: The last scanned QR code value. Empty string until the first successful scan.

variables.name: cameras
variables.description: Array of available camera device labels.

variables.name: hasCamera
variables.description: True when a camera is available and the current cameraSelection/cameraName configuration resolves to a usable camera.

specifications: Renders as a self-closing element with no children or slots; the video preview is injected by html5-qrcode into the element's container.
Must be wrapped in a ww-form-container when used as a form input — fieldName, required, customValidation and validation only take effect inside a form.
When using cameraSelection='custom', also set cameraName to a camera label or device ID. Leave cameraName empty for 'environment' or 'user'.
Do not rely on the 'status' variable auto-resetting — it stays 'success' after a successful scan until the next scan event.
Size the element via styles (width/height) — the scanner refreshes on element resize, and a 0-sized element will not start scanning.
